title: Implement policy-based MemorySmith writer service for durable world observations
description:
Create a policy-driven service that persists only meaningful world observations to MemorySmith (World KB), avoiding the noise of every-tick writes.

Implementation:
1. Create `Agent.Memory/WorldObservationWriter.cs` (or similar):
   - Takes `IMemoryGateway` (keyed "world") for writing to World KB.
   - Writing policy: only persist on meaningful boundaries, not every cycle.
2. Define write triggers (configurable):
   - New landmark discovered (unique location, significant biome change)
   - Goal started / completed / failed
   - Action failed with a new error pattern
   - Explicit user checkpoint request
   - Recurring failure pattern (same action fails 3+ times)
   - Scan result worth reusing (unique block distribution, structure found)
3. Define page types and slugs:
   - `snapshot/{yyyy-MM-dd-HHmmss}` — significant scene snapshots
   - `landmark/{name}` — stable location records (deduplicate by position proximity)
   - `goal/{goalId}` — persistent goal context
   - `failure/{action}` — recurring failure patterns (update existing page, don't create new)
4. Each page includes: timestamp, position, relevant goal, key facts, scene summary.
5. Deduplication: check if a similar page exists (same landmark within 10 blocks, same failure pattern) before creating a new one.

Acceptance:
- Writes are event-driven, not timer-driven.
- No writes during normal idle loops.
- Landmark pages are deduplicated.
- Failure pages accumulate patterns without creating N copies.

Refs: `memorysmith_situational_awareness_design_doc_20260625T020914Z.md` Phase 2
